Minimum report: Name the organ-matched atlas annotation version pathway definitions and any population skew that affects interpretation
Common failure if omitted: Atlas claims appear stronger or more general than the underlying reference supports
LEMPA lesson: The lung atlas layer was strongest when framed as an adult-skewed normal-lung resource not as a universal lifespan map

Minimum report: Report source cohort counts exclusions final paired N and one-to-one linkage rules
Common failure if omitted: Readers cannot tell whether the predictive benchmark used a real paired cohort or a drifting subset
LEMPA lesson: LEMPA needed exact counts from 211 source imaging cases to 130 paired patients to keep the benchmark interpretable

Minimum report: List imaging predictors transcriptomic targets covariates and whether each endpoint was preregistered or fallback-derived
Common failure if omitted: Residual associations may be mistaken for original primary endpoints
LEMPA lesson: H4b became high-risk because its V2 target depended on module collapse rather than the original V1 heterogeneity construct

Minimum report: Show at least one cross-method agreement summary before downstream prediction
Common failure if omitted: Weak targets are treated as ground truth and downstream failures are misattributed
LEMPA lesson: V2-G2 failure showed that target instability can invalidate the intended cell-type validation surface before modeling

Minimum report: Declare in advance when targets will be collapsed analyses skipped or a line archived
Common failure if omitted: Post hoc salvage steps can be misread as planned optimization
LEMPA lesson: Module collapse to epithelial immune endothelial and stromal was only interpretable because the fallback was explicit

Minimum report: State the exact performance metric threshold and success rule before showing results
Common failure if omitted: Negative outcomes are softened into trends or selective examples
LEMPA lesson: V1 remained negative because the binding rule was >=10 cell types at r >= 0.30 not a best-cell-type narrative

Minimum report: Report whether site batch histology or external-cohort splits were feasible and what happened if they were not
Common failure if omitted: Internal performance is overgeneralized as transportable validation
LEMPA lesson: V2-G5 had to stay SKIPPED because frozen local strata could not support a real held-out test

Minimum report: Report nulls alongside positive-looking remainders and state whether multiple-testing correction was used
Common failure if omitted: Significant leftovers can overshadow the actual negative main result
LEMPA lesson: H4a and H4c stayed null which prevented H4b from being presented as broad biologic confirmation

Minimum report: Map each display item to exact frozen inputs and generation logic
Common failure if omitted: Visual summaries become harder to audit than the text
LEMPA lesson: The BIB package needed an explicit figure/table plan so the practitioner story stayed evidence-linked

Minimum report: Report software constraints scoring substitutions and whether the executed stack differs from the ideal stack
Common failure if omitted: Readers assume a reproducibility grade that the run did not actually meet
LEMPA lesson: LEMPA had to state that pathway scoring used gseapy.ssgsea rather than canonical GSVA in the local environment

Minimum report: Say explicitly whether an independent paired cohort existed and whether replication was achieved
Common failure if omitted: Stress-test results are mistaken for validated transportable models
LEMPA lesson: No external paired cohort meant the broad line had to be archived rather than promoted

Minimum report: If a line is deferred state what future evidence would justify reopening it
Common failure if omitted: Failed programs linger as implicit future promises
LEMPA lesson: Phase C remained dormant until trigger-grade paired resources or reproducibility-grade method restoration become available
